page.title: Hypothyroidism — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Hypothyroidism: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Hypothyroidism: 159 alterations and 81 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Exposure to high-fluoride drinking water appears to non-linearly affect thyroid function and increase TSH release in children.
Chronic exposure to organochlorine compounds may contribute to type 2 diabetes and thyroid diseases.

Safety Study: There is little evidence that in those with normal thyroid function soy has any adverse effects. In those with hypothyroidism adequate iodine should be consumed to counterbalance the soy consumption.
A number of commonly used drugs interfere with the absorption of levothyroxine.
A prospective study to evaluate the possible role of cholecalciferol supplementation on autoimmunity in Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
Aromatherapy reduces fatigue among women with hypothyroidism.
Associations between exposure to phthalates and subclinical hypothyroidism in pregnant women during early pregnancy.
Bisphenol A and pthalate exposure may adversely affect thyroid function.
Carnitine supplementation may correct carnitine deficiences associated with both hypo- and hyperthyroidism.
Congenital hypothyroidism and mental retardation may be linked to iodine and trace mineral deficiencies.
Desiccated thyroid extract compares favorably with levothyroxine in the treatment of hypothyroidism.
Dessicated thyroid (Armour) has been associated with superior clinical outcomes compared to levothyroxine in the treatment of hypothyroidism.
Di(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate metabolites may alter thyroid hormone levels in men.
Effect of 12 weeks yoga therapy on heart rate variability and baroreflex sensitivity in female hypothyroid patients.
Effect of selenium on anti-Tg antibody in patients with autoimmune hypothyroidism.
Effect of yoga on depression in hypothyroidism: A pilot study.
Effects of exposure to polychlorinated biphenyls and organochlorine pesticides have an adverse effect on thyroid function during pregnancy.
Exposure to organochloride compounds alter thyroid hormone levels during pregnancy.
Fluoride exposure and indicators of thyroid functioning in the Canadian population.
Fluoride in drinking water was associated with increased risk of hypothyroidism in pregnant women.
Gamma-oryzanol produces a signficant reduction in elevated TSH levels in hypothyroid patients, possibly by a direct action on the hypothalamus.
Ginseng improves the condition of patients with congestive heart failure and raises levels of T3 and T4.

he degree of individuality measured in the plasma hormones is such that conventional population-based reference ranges may not correctly identify major alterations in these hormones in individual subjects.
Homeopathic intervention was beneficial in subclinical hypothyroidism with or without autoimmune thyroiditis in children.
Hypothyroidism is a protective factor in acute stroke patients.

In hypothyroid patients L-T4-replacement, that is sufficient to maintain a normal serum TSH, is accompanied by a serum free T4 that is higher than that in untreated euthyroid patients or normal individuals and may not result in an appropriately normal ser
In thyroid replacement resistant hypothyroidism autoimmunity and gastroenterologic pathologies are common.
Infants with congenital hypothyroidism may do well to avoid soy-based formula.
Intolerance to wheat (CD autoantibodies) is higher than background rates in those with Turner syndrome.
Iodine deficiency is a major determinant in the occurence of thyroid underfunction in pregnant women.
Iodine deficiency may be a common problem in premature newborns.
Isotretinoin, a widely prescribed anti-acne drug, significantly alters serum thyroid parameters.
Levothyroxine may be a risk factor for hypoglycaemia in patients with liver disease.
Levothyroxine replacement therapy increases coagulation (fibrinogen) following initiation of treatment.
Levothyroxine use is associated with increased risk of hypoglycemia in patients with liver impairment.

Long-term exposure to an electromagnetic field affects bone and thyroid metabolism and also increases oxidative stress index by increasing the total oxidant status and decreasing the antioxidant status.
Magnesium prevents exercise-induced reduction in thyroid hormone activity in both sedentary and athletic individuals.
Non-malignant thyroid disease may occur after a wide range of radiation exposures
Organochlorine compound exposures may disrupt thyroid function, resulting in neurotoxicity in preschool children.
Organophosphate pesticide exposure has been associated with thyroid hormone changes in human subjects.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Hashimoto's (most common cause) is irreversible in majority; spontaneous recovery in <5%; subclinical hypothyroidism spontaneously normalizes in ~30-40% of cases within 2 years without treatment
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: T4 monotherapy normalizes TSH in nearly all patients and resolves symptoms in ~60%; up to 40% of T4-treated patients remain symptomatic despite normalized TSH; T4+T3 combination or desiccated thyroid extract (DTE) preferred by ~50% of patients in blinded preference trials
remission.gap_size: Moderate — within the treated population, a large minority are inadequately treated by the standard-of-care T4 monotherapy approach
remission.barrier_detail: Up to 40% of patients remain symptomatic on T4 despite guideline-defined adequate treatment; T3 combination and DTE are rarely offered in primary care despite guideline acknowledgment that a trial may be appropriate; TSH-only monitoring misses tissue-level hypothyroidism
remission.last_soc_change: 2014 — ATA guidelines last updated; acknowledged T4/T3 combination as appropriate trial in symptomatic patients; 2019 ETA/BTA guidelines more explicitly acknowledged need for personalized approach; T4 monotherapy has been standard since 1980s displacement of DTE
remission.outdated_treatments: TSH-only monitoring without free T4 and T3 measurement in symptomatic patients; rigid adherence to T4 monotherapy in symptomatic patients without considering combination or DTE; desiccated thyroid extract (DTE) was the only treatment for 110+ years before being discarded largely for convenience reasons rather than evidence of inferiority
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Selenium supplementation (reduces anti-TPO antibodies in Hashimoto's, may slow progression); low-dose naltrexone (LDN) in autoimmune thyroid disease; vitamin D optimization; gluten-free diet in Hashimoto's with coexistent celiac or non-celiac gluten sensitivity
remission.frontier_signal: Thyroid regeneration via stem cells (early animal models); mRNA-based thyroid hormone delivery; immune tolerization for Hashimoto's prevention; thyroid-stimulating hormone receptor antibody (TRAb) targeted therapy for Graves' disease
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
